Star Wars: A New Hope is an action-packed adventure filled with laughs and great fun. This definitely has some shotty CGI, but that was expected. Harrison Ford, Mark Hamill, Carrie Fisher, and many more great actors brought this to life in more ways than one. It does have some moments that feel a little drawn out, but all in all, it’s great, it’s fun, and keeps you entertained.

Não é apenas um filme; é o surgimento de um marco cultural que redefiniu, de forma definitiva, a relação entre cinema, imaginação e espetáculo coletivo. George Lucas concebe um universo que parece sempre maior do que a própria tela, onde cada planeta, criatura e detalhe visual sugere uma vastidão narrativa que extrapola o filme em si — um mundo que continua existindo mesmo quando a projeção termina. Desde o instante em que a trilha monumental de John Williams irrompe, anunciando a grandiosidade do que está por vir, até o ataque final à Estrela da Morte, tudo pulsa com a energia de um cinema que acredita genuinamente no heroísmo, na aventura e, sobretudo, na esperança. Mark Hamill, Harrison Ford e Carrie Fisher não apenas interpretam personagens: eles dão origem a figuras arquetípicas. Que ultrapassam rapidamente o estatuto de protagonistas para se tornarem ícones eternos da cultura pop. Um verdadeiro patrimônio da sétima arte.

★★★★★ Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope is the once-upon-a-time that changed everything: a perfect, lightning-in-a-bottle fairy tale that somehow feels both timeless and like it was made yesterday. From the very first shot of that Star Destroyer roaring overhead (still one of the greatest entrances in cinema history) to the triumphant medal ceremony, every single minute crackles with pure, wide-eyed wonder. George Lucas didn’t just make a movie in 1977; he created an entire universe in two hours and felt like it had always existed. Tatooine’s twin suns, the cantina band, the trash-compactor panic, "These aren’t the droids you’re looking for," the swing across the chasm, the Death Star trench run; these are cultural DNA now. The cast is magic: Mark Hamill’s farm-boy sincerity, Harrison Ford inventing cool with a smirk and a blaster, Carrie Fisher’s razor-sharp princess who can out-sass anyone, Alec Guinness giving Obi-Wan the weight of a thousand untold stories, and of course the greatest duo in film history: a walking carpet and a golden trash can with anxiety. John Williams’ score isn’t just music; it’s the sound of hope itself. The effects were revolutionary then and still look gorgeous today because they were real models, real explosions, real heart. And that final shot of Luke looking at the binary sunset… forty-eight years later it still makes grown adults tear up in the dark. It’s simple, it’s sincere, it’s funny, it’s thrilling, and it believes with every frame that good can win if ordinary people stand up. No cynicism, no irony, just pure heroic myth wearing a used spacesuit. A New Hope didn’t just start a franchise. It started modern blockbuster cinema, cosplay, fandom as we know it, and the dreams of generations. Flawless. Life-changing. The definition of 10/10. A long time ago in a galaxy far, far away… thank you for everything. 🌟⚔️🚀

Last time I watched Star Wars it was brand new in theaters :) I had been to Washington DC in 1976 to celebrate the bicentennial. We went to NASA during that family vacation, I was fascinated! Then to see Star Wars a year later, I couldn’t have enjoyed it more. I tried to take myself back in time while watching the Star Wars trilogy today in 2025. The story is excellent, its humor is timeless and the special effects are impressive considering they’re almost 40 years old. I also liked how the Darth Vader, the bad guy, was just that- not some totally whacked out, demented, gruesome killer, you often get these days. I loved it! 5 STARS

There’s a constant stream of opinions and conversations about Star Wars on the internet—positive, negative, insightful, toxic, but ultimately endless. Discussions about the prequel trilogy, the sequel trilogy, the spinoff movies, the animated shows, the live-action series, the games, the comics, the books. It’s easy to forget that it all began with one film. One film that changed everything. One film that changed cinema. One film that changed the world. One film that changed history. I put this on as a palate cleanser, a comfort film—something I trusted, something fun and easy to watch, something I would truly enjoy. And it made me smile. I loved it. It’s fun, it’s comfort, it’s cinema, it’s movie magic, it’s storytelling at its best. It’s everything.
